Identity.Name: StingHVACPressureClasses
Identity.Definition: HVAC duct pressure classes per BSRIA DW/144 + SMACNA — selects the duct construction standard, gauge, seam type, and leakage class for a given system maximum static pressure. Drives MepAutoSizeDuctCommand sizing decisions and SeamAudit / GaugeBreakpoint resolution in the STING_MEP_SIZING_RULES.json registry.

Governance.StandardsBasis: BSRIA DW/144 (UK); SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards (US); EN 1507 / EN 12237 (EU)

Value.Definition: Class A — up to 500 Pa static pressure. Low-pressure ducting, light-gauge construction, lowest leakage class.
Class B — up to 1000 Pa. Medium-pressure ducting; standard supply / return systems.
Class C — up to 2500 Pa. High-pressure ducting; long runs, high-velocity systems, plant-room mains.
Class D — up to 7500 Pa. Very high pressure; smoke control + clean rooms + specialised process exhaust.
